appraisal_events.evidence: « répondit Swann qui savait bien pourtant par expérience que certains ne l’étaient pas, mais qui, comme tous les gens qui ont une opinion ardente, aimait mieux … leur supposer une raison préconçue »
appraisal_events.explanation: The narrator criticizes Swann's generalization (attributing anti-Dreyfusism to the antisemitism of 'all those people'), presenting it as a bias of polemical fervor rather than an even-handed judgment.

status_effects.explanation: The narrator locally belittles him by highlighting his tendency to partisan generalization concerning the Guermantes and the Dreyfus affair.
